theory.concept:
CROSS JOIN pairs every row from one table with every row from another — no matching condition at all, just every possible combination.

- The result size is the number of rows in table1 multiplied by the number of rows in table2 — this grows fast, so it's rarely used on large tables directly
- Useful for generating combinations that don't naturally exist in your data yet — e.g. every product paired with every size, before you know which combinations actually sold
- Different from INNER JOIN with no ON clause producing the same result by accident — CROSS JOIN is explicit about the intent
- You can still add WHERE after a CROSS JOIN to narrow the combinations down to ones you actually care about
theory.syntax: SELECT columns FROM table1 CROSS JOIN table2;

example.query:
-- 1. Every student paired with every course offered
SELECT s.name, c.course_name FROM Students AS s CROSS JOIN Courses AS c;

shared_table.name: Students
Courses

questions.prompt: Count how many total student-course combinations exist.
List only the combinations involving 'Priya' or the course 'Networks'.
